target.title: Bug: bridge-backed `capture live` fails with opaque `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1`
target.body:
# Bug: bridge-backed `capture live` fails with opaque `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1`

Public submission status: local draft only, not submitted.

## Summary

When `peekaboo capture live` uses the default bridge-backed runtime in a clean Tart macOS guest, multiple capture modes fail immediately with:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR",
    "message": "The operation couldn't be completed. (PeekabooBridge.PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1.)"
  }
}
```

The error is too opaque for an agent or CLI user to determine whether the failure is caused by permissions, bridge version skew, unsupported capture mode, daemon state, or a real capture bug. The same source-built binary can successfully produce live capture artifacts through local/in-process capture paths, so this does not appear to be a blanket inability to capture video in the guest.

## Environment

- Product: Peekaboo CLI
- Version: `Peekaboo 3.2.3 (main/122c96da, built: 2026-05-31T21:02:24Z)`
- Tested commit: `122c96da0bd7909df5ca4d3dcd98e086c8bd0733`
- Test host: Tart VM from base `menubar-sequoia-xcode-peekaboo`
- Guest OS: macOS 15.7.3 build 24G419
- Xcode: 26.4 build 17E192
- Run id: `peekaboo-video-docs-20260531T205619Z`
- Important constraint: host desktop capture was not used as pass evidence; all pass/fail evidence came from inside the guest.

The CLI reported bridge-backed runtime startup like this:

```text
Runtime host: remote onDemand via .../Library/Application Support/Peekaboo/bridge.sock (build 3.1.1 (3.1.1))
```

The source-built CLI binary under test reported `3.2.3`, while the bridge log line reported bridge build `3.1.1`. I do not know whether that version mismatch is causal, but it is a strong clue to inspect.

## Reproduction

From a source-built Peekaboo checkout at commit `122c96da0bd7909df5ca4d3dcd98e086c8bd0733`, run the default bridge-backed live capture commands in a clean macOS guest:

```bash
RUN_ROOT="$HOME/peekaboo-video-runs/peekaboo-video-docs-20260531T205619Z"
PEEKABOO="$RUN_ROOT/Peekaboo/Apps/CLI/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/peekaboo"

"$PEEKABOO" capture live \
  --mode screen \
  --screen-index 0 \
  --duration 5 \
  --active-fps 8 \
  --threshold 0 \
  --path "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/screen" \
  --video-out "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/screen.mp4" \
  --json

"$PEEKABOO" capture live \
  --mode frontmost \
  --duration 5 \
  --active-fps 8 \
  --threshold 0 \
  --path "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/frontmost" \
  --video-out "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/frontmost.mp4" \
  --json

"$PEEKABOO" capture live \
  --mode area \
  --region 100,100,640,360 \
  --duration 5 \
  --active-fps 8 \
  --threshold 0 \
  --path "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/area" \
  --video-out "$RUN_ROOT/baseline/area.mp4" \
  --json
```

I also reproduced the same opaque bridge error in these stress cases:

- `screen-static`: `capture live --mode screen --duration 4 --active-fps 4 --threshold 0.8`
- `area-motion`: `capture live --mode area --region 100,100,640,360 --duration 5 --active-fps 8 --threshold 0`
- `frontmost-window`: `capture live --mode frontmost --duration 4 --active-fps 8 --threshold 0`
- `max-frames`: `capture live --mode area --region 100,100,640,360 --duration 10 --active-fps 8 --threshold 0 --max-frames 2`
- `post-timeout-recovery`: `capture live --mode area --region 100,100,640,360 --duration 4 --active-fps 8 --threshold 0`

## Expected Behavior

One of the following should happen:

- Bridge-backed `capture live` succeeds and writes the requested MP4, frame, contact-sheet, and metadata artifacts.
- If the bridge cannot perform the request, the CLI returns a specific actionable error code and message, for example a permission failure, unsupported mode, bridge startup/version mismatch, bridge daemon failure, or capture-engine failure.
- If the bridge build is incompatible with the CLI build, the CLI detects that and reports it directly instead of surfacing a generic internal Swift error.

## Actual Behavior

All default bridge-backed baseline cases failed quickly, with no artifacts:

| Case | Mode | Exit | Duration | Artifacts | Error |
|---|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| `screen` | `screen` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `frontmost` | `frontmost` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `area` | `area` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `screen-static` | `screen` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `area-motion` | `area` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `frontmost-window` | `frontmost` | 1 | 2s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `max-frames` | `area` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |
| `post-timeout-recovery` | `area` | 1 | 1s | none | `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR`, `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` |

Representative JSON output from the `screen` case:

```json
{
  "debug_logs": [
    "Runtime host: remote onDemand via .../Library/Application Support/Peekaboo/bridge.sock (build 3.1.1 (3.1.1))",
    "Starting operation {mode=screen, operation=capture_live}",
    "Starting timer 'capture_live'"
  ],
  "error": {
    "code": "INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR",
    "message": "The operation couldn't be completed. (PeekabooBridge.PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1.)"
  },
  "success": false
}
```

## Control Case: Local Capture Works

The same source-built binary successfully produced guest-local live capture artifacts when forced to use local/in-process capture paths:

```bash
"$PEEKABOO" capture live \
  --mode area \
  --region 100,100,640,360 \
  --capture-engine cg \
  --duration 4 \
  --active-fps 8 \
  --threshold 0 \
  --path "$RUN_ROOT/stress/engine-cg" \
  --video-out "$RUN_ROOT/stress/engine-cg.mp4" \
  --json

"$PEEKABOO" capture live \
  --no-remote \
  --mode area \
  --region 100,100,640,360 \
  --duration 4 \
  --active-fps 8 \
  --threshold 0 \
  --path "$RUN_ROOT/stress/no-remote-local" \
  --video-out "$RUN_ROOT/stress/no-remote-local.mp4" \
  --json
```

Both control cases exited `0`. Each produced:

- MP4: about 417 KB
- Contact sheet: about 221 KB
- Metadata JSON: about 3 KB
- 10 kept frame PNGs
- `framesDropped: 0`
- `warnings: []`

This suggests the bridge-backed path is the failing surface, or at minimum that the bridge-backed path is hiding the real underlying reason.

## Impact

This blocks reliable agent verification workflows. An agent needs to know whether live capture failed because of a fixable setup condition, a permissions condition, a bridge daemon/version condition, or a real capture bug. The current `INTERNAL_SWIFT_ERROR` message is not actionable enough to recover automatically.

## Suggested Fix

- Preserve the original bridge error code/domain/details when converting bridge errors into CLI JSON.
- Detect and report bridge/CLI version mismatches explicitly.
- Include which runtime path was selected: remote bridge, local/in-process, capture engine, and fallback decisions.
- Add a regression test for bridge error-envelope translation so `PeekabooBridgeErrorEnvelope error 1` cannot escape as the final user-facing message without additional context.
target.author: coygeek
